title: Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple
tagline: Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple is a Hindu temple located in the heart of Erumely town in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple is a Hindu temple located in the heart of Erumely town in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The temple is dedicated to Ayyappa or Dharmasastha. It is also an important meeting place of Sabarimala pilgrims.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Erumely
topPlaces.description: Erumely, also spelled Erumeli, is a town and panchayat located in the south-eastern part of Kottayam district in Kerala, India. Erumely is situated 49 km (30 mi) east of Kottayam town and 133 km (83 mi) north of the state capital Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Chunkappara
topPlaces.description: Chunkappara is a small town in the Pathanamthitta district of Kerala state, South India. Chunkappara is situated 26 km (16 mi) east of Thiruvalla, where the nearest railway station is located.It is part of Thiruvalla Revenue Division & Thiruvalla Constituency.Other neighbouring towns include Kanjirappally, Manimala, Ranny, Mallappally, Karukachal and Erumely.

topPlaces.name: Kadayanickadu
topPlaces.description: Kadayanickadu is a village situated 11 km east of Karukachal, spread out on either sides of Changanacherry - Manimala road in Kerala, India. It is a typical Kerala rural settlement with the nature's green covering, ringing rivulets, small hills and slopes, agricultural fields, inhabited by rustic population.

topPlaces.name: Kottangal
topPlaces.description: Kottangal is a census village in Kottangal gram panchayat in the Mallapally taluk, Thiruvalla Revenue Division in the district of Pathanamthitta in Kerala.It is part of Thiruvalla Constituency.As of 2011, it had a population of 17,174. The Kottangal Devi Temple is famous for its Padayani performances- an art form dedicated to the Hindu goddess Bhadrakali, unique to the Central Travancore region.

topPlaces.name: Vellavoor
topPlaces.description: Vellavoor is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Cheruvally Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: The Cheruvally Devi Temple is located in the village of Cheruvally in the Kanjirappally taluk of Kottayam, Kerala state, India. The presiding deity here is Goddess Bhadrakali, a more fierce form of [Devi Aadishakthi.

topPlaces.name: Perumpetty
topPlaces.description: Perumpetty is a census village in Kottanad gram panchayat in the Mallapally taluk of the district of Pathanamthitta in Kerala. As of 2011, it had a population of 14,396.

topPlaces.name: Chenappady
topPlaces.description: Chenappady is a small village on the foothills of the Sahyadri of Kerala, in the banks of Manimala River. It is located under the Kanjirappally locality which is 8 km (5.0 mi) from there.

items.note: Erumely, also spelled Erumeli, is a town and panchayat located in the south-eastern part of Kottayam district in Kerala, India. Erumely is situated 49 km (30 mi) east of Kottayam town and 133 km (83 mi) north of the state capital Thiruvananthapuram.

Chunkappara is a small town in the Pathanamthitta district of Kerala state, South India. Chunkappara is situated 26 km (16 mi) east of Thiruvalla, where the nearest railway station is located.It is part of Thiruvalla Revenue Division & Thiruvalla Constituency.Other neighbouring towns include Kanjirappally, Manimala, Ranny, Mallappally, Karukachal and Erumely.

Kadayanickadu is a village situated 11 km east of Karukachal, spread out on either sides of Changanacherry - Manimala road in Kerala, India. It is a typical Kerala rural settlement with the nature's green covering, ringing rivulets, small hills and slopes, agricultural fields, inhabited by rustic population.

Kottangal is a census village in Kottangal gram panchayat in the Mallapally taluk, Thiruvalla Revenue Division in the district of Pathanamthitta in Kerala.It is part of Thiruvalla Constituency.As of 2011, it had a population of 17,174. The Kottangal Devi Temple is famous for its Padayani performances- an art form dedicated to the Hindu goddess Bhadrakali, unique to the Central Travancore region.
Vellavoor is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India.

The Cheruvally Devi Temple is located in the village of Cheruvally in the Kanjirappally taluk of Kottayam, Kerala state, India. The presiding deity here is Goddess Bhadrakali, a more fierce form of [Devi Aadishakthi.
Perumpetty is a census village in Kottanad gram panchayat in the Mallapally taluk of the district of Pathanamthitta in Kerala. As of 2011, it had a population of 14,396.
Chenappady is a small village on the foothills of the Sahyadri of Kerala, in the banks of Manimala River. It is located under the Kanjirappally locality which is 8 km (5.0 mi) from there.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (86 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (76 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Erumely Sree Dharmasastha Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Erumely, Chunkappara, Kadayanickadu, Kottangal, Vellavoor.
